title: Memory Object
description: Layer-2 representation in the three-layer memory architecture. The editorially compressed, structured form of a layer-1 raw artifact, retaining provenance back to the original.

id.description: Stable identifier. UUID v4 or content-derived hash. Must be unique within the memory store.

source_uri.description: Resolvable pointer to the layer-1 raw artifact. Must dereference at write time.

source_kind.description: Origin classifier (open vocabulary). Recommended values: slack_thread, gmail_thread, gmeet_transcript, granola_note, transcript_file, deck, doc, manual_note.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the memory object was written (not when the source was created).

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when this memory object should be pruned/demoted, or null for no expiry.

kind.description: Content classifier driving expiry windows and retrieval ranking (open vocabulary). Recommended values: strategy, weekly, retrospective, decision, legal, thread, note, glossary_term.

title.description: Optional human-readable title for retrieval display.

summary.description: Optional one-sentence summary distinct from body.

event_id.description: Optional. Cluster id (per #10 — multi-fidelity event matching). Memory objects representing the same real-world event share an event_id; absent for unclustered items.

is_canonical_for_event.description: Optional. True if this memory is the canonical (highest-authority) member of its event cluster. At most one canonical per event_id; the rest are alternates with `superseded_by` pointing at this memory's id.

superseded_by.description: Optional. When this memory is an alternate in a multi-source cluster, points at the canonical memory's id. Null/absent when this memory is canonical (or unclustered).

expiry_locked.description: Optional. When true, prune.py respects the user-set expires_at and will NOT auto-archive this memory regardless of expiry policy. F3 mitigation from issue #8.
